The Lord’s servants are deeply troubled by seeing ungodliness and violence prevail, especially among those who profess the truth. No one hesitated doing wrong to his neighbor. We long to move to a world where holiness and love reign forever, and no violence will be before us. God has good reasons for his patience toward bad people, and the rebukes of good people. The day will come when the cry of sin will be heard against those who do wrong, and the cry of prayer for those who suffer wrong. They were to notice what was happening among the Gentiles by the Chaldeans, and to consider themselves a nation to be punished by them. But most people assume continued prosperity, or that disasters will not come in their days. They are a bitter and hasty nation, fierce, cruel, and pushing down all before them. They will overcome all who oppose them. But it is a great offense, and the common offense of proud people, to take glory to themselves. The closing words give a glimpse of comfort.

However things may be, yet God is the Lord our God, our Holy One. We are an offending people; he is an offended God, yet we will not have harsh thoughts of him, or of his service. It is great comfort that, whatever harm people plan, the Lord plans good, and we are sure that his purpose will stand. Though wickedness may prosper for a while, yet God is holy, and does not approve of wickedness. Because he cannot do evil himself, he is too pure to look on it with approval. By this principle we must abide, though the ways of his providence may for a time, in some cases, seem to us not to agree with it. The prophet complains that God’s patience was abused; and because judgment against these evil works and workers was not carried out quickly, their hearts were more fully set on doing evil. Some they take up one by one like fish on a hook; others they catch in shoals, as in their net, and gather them in their drag-net. They admire their own cleverness and planning: there is great tendency in us to take the glory of outward prosperity for ourselves. This is idolizing ourselves, sacrificing to the drag-net because it is our own. God will soon end successful and splendid robberies. Death and judgment will make people cease preying on others, and they will be preyed on themselves. Let us remember that whatever advantages we possess, we must give all the glory to God.
